The extracts of Carissa tetramera have been studied for their potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. These properties are believed to contribute to the plant's traditional uses in folk medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. intro: Carissa tetramera, also known as the 'White Bird of Paradise', is a flowering plant species in the Apocynaceae family native to South Africa. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Carissa tetramera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its health benefits.
